draft.title: ai workspace
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
ai workspace

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: The user prefers warm, diffused lighting over harsh contrast in interface design
facade.label: Soft ambient light
facade.acceptImplies: Explore interfaces with gentle gradients, warm color temperatures, and soft shadows that reduce eye strain
facade.rejectImplies: Test sharper contrast, cooler tones, and stronger visual hierarchy through bold lighting differences

facade.hypothesis: The user gravitates toward approachable, non-intimidating interfaces rather than sharp, technical aesthetics
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: Workspace should feel inviting and human-centered, not sterile or aggressive. Curves over edges.
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer precision and clarity signaled through defined geometry. Sharp corners, grids, and structured forms feel more trustworthy or professional.

evidence.hypothesis: The user prefers warm, diffused lighting over harsh contrast in interface design
evidence.implication: Explore interfaces with gentle gradients, warm color temperatures, and soft shadows that reduce eye strain

facade.hypothesis: Users building an AI workspace prefer dense, scannable data visualization over narrative explanations
facade.axisTargeted: Information density — sparse vs. dense presentation
facade.label: Data dashboard
facade.acceptImplies: Workspace should surface key metrics, charts, and live stats prominently. Real-time data readability matters more than storytelling.
facade.rejectImplies: Workspace should favor clear explanations, guided narratives, and prose-based insights. Users want context and meaning over raw numbers.

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer conversational, approachable language over corporate formality in their AI workspace
facade.label: Casual chat tone
facade.acceptImplies: The workspace should feel like talking to a helpful colleague, not reading documentation
facade.rejectImplies: The workspace should maintain clear professional boundaries and formal language patterns

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer direct manipulation (drag-drop) over menu-based organization in workspace tools
facade.axisTargeted: Motion and Behavior — gesture-driven interaction vs. click-through dialogs
facade.label: Drag to arrange
facade.acceptImplies: Workspace layout should reward fluid, tactile rearrangement; smooth animations on drop; direct visual feedback
facade.rejectImplies: Workspace prefers explicit actions (buttons, menus, confirmations); instant feedback without transition; spatial changes feel deliberate, not fluid

facade.hypothesis: Users of AI workspaces prefer vertical navigation paired with modular card layouts for task organization
facade.label: Sidebar + cards
facade.acceptImplies: Explore more sidebar-heavy designs with card-based content systems; test depth of sidebar customization
facade.rejectImplies: Test tab-based or top-nav alternatives; explore list-based layouts over cards; consider more spacious, fewer-per-view designs

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface layout
Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs neutral/professional communication style
Whether user wants a calm/focused atmosphere vs energetic/stimulating workspace mood
Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards vs simplified summaries and key metrics only
Whether user likes instant feedback and snappy responses vs smooth, deliberate transitions and animations

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface layout
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs neutral/professional communication style
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants a calm/focused atmosphere vs energetic/stimulating workspace mood
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards vs simplified summaries and key metrics only
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user likes instant feedback and snappy responses vs smooth, deliberate transitions and animations
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: The user prefers minimal color schemes that reduce cognitive load and create a calm, focused workspace environment
facade.label: Monochrome palette
facade.acceptImplies: Explore interfaces with limited color palettes (grays, blacks, whites with one accent color) to maintain clarity and reduce visual noise in the AI workspace
facade.rejectImplies: The user values color richness and visual variety—explore interfaces with varied, expressive color systems that help distinguish between different AI tools and outputs

draft.title: AI Workspace Hub
draft.summary: A focused workspace interface for managing AI tasks, projects, and collaborative workflows. Clean, minimal design with warm neutrals and intuitive navigation.
